Military Mar 29, 2026

6 Military Aircraft With The Most Complex Avionics

The flying supercomputers of 2026.

Modern military aircraft avionics integrate AI-driven sensor fusion and autonomous decision-making systems that commercial aviation is beginning to adopt for enhanced safety and efficiency.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Why Do Airlines Keep Older Aircraft In Service Longer Than Expected?

Older aircraft come with higher operating costs, but have unique capabilities and have already been paid off by their operator.

Airlines extend older aircraft lifespans because fully depreciated assets provide better economics than new aircraft despite higher maintenance costs, especially when those planes fill niche route requirements.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 28, 2026

JetBlue Passenger With Highest Mosaic Status Caught Performing Inappropriate Act Inflight

The ACARS message sent during JetBlue Flight 2858 revealed a shocking act taking place in the cabin, involving a passenger in Mint.

Airline loyalty status offers no protection against enforcement of cabin conduct standards, signaling consistent application of behavioral policies regardless of customer tier.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 28, 2026

After 20 Years, The Airbus A380 Still Shapes Airport Design

A closer look at some of the upgrades that airports had to make in order to accommodate the superjumbo.

The A380's infrastructure demands established design standards that continue influencing airport planning decades after production ended, creating lasting competitive advantages for early adopters.

See Simple Flying →

PAI
Safety Mar 29, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective safety risk management requires organizations to document why selected controls are proportionate to identified hazards and justify acceptance of remaining risks rather than pursuing elimination.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Commercial Mar 28, 2026

5 Airlines With The Largest Boeing 787 Fleets In 2026

The aircraft is becoming the backbone of many airlines' fleets.

The 787's dominance signals a shift toward fuel-efficient wide-body operations on long-haul routes as airlines prioritize sustainability and lower per-seat costs.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 28, 2026

Just 222 Seats: United Airlines' 4 Ultra-Premium Boeing 787 Routes In 2026

The new layout will initially be deployed domestically, with its international rollout coming next month.

United's ultra-premium 787 configuration signals a shift toward high-yield domestic routes, potentially increasing ancillary revenue per flight despite reduced seat capacity.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 28, 2026

5 US Airports Undergoing Major Improvement Projects

These facilities are continuing to expand.

Strategic infrastructure investments at these five hubs signal operators' confidence in sustained demand recovery and capacity constraints post-pandemic.

Military Mar 28, 2026

Northrop Grumman Testbed With AIM-260-Like Nose Spotted Flying Alongside Raytheon’s VOODOO 1 at Eglin AFB

The two testbeds, known by their callsigns SCAN 06 and VOODOO 1, carried out concurrent operations in Eglin’s highly instrumented airspace. For three consecutive days, on Mar. 24, 25 and 26, two testbed aircraft, the Northrop Grumman CRJ-700 carrying the U.S. registration code N806X and typically...

Concurrent testing of advanced air-to-air missile systems by competing contractors suggests accelerated development timelines for next-generation combat aircraft armament.

See The Aviationist →

Regulatory Mar 29, 2026

How to Assess Safety Risk

A practical walkthrough of safety risk assessment under 14 CFR Part 5.

Part 5's structured risk assessment framework enables operators to systematically quantify hazards and their mitigation effectiveness before incidents occur.

Commercial Mar 28, 2026

Why Airlines Choose Certain Aircraft For Routes

An almost endless array of factors must be considered when deciding which plane should fly where...

Route profitability depends on matching aircraft capacity, fuel efficiency, and turnaround times to specific demand patterns and airport constraints rather than simply deploying available equipment.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 28, 2026

Starfish Space finds a new partner for docking demonstration mission

Starfish Space has changed plans for a docking demonstration mission after its original partner backed out.

Starfish Space's pivot to a new partner signals the critical importance of redundancy in on-orbit servicing partnerships, where single-supplier dependencies can derail commercial space logistics timelines.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 28, 2026

Germany Considering MQ-28A Ghost Bat CCA, Says Defense Minister

German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius said the MQ-28 Ghost Bat is among the uncrewed aircraft being evaluated to support the Eurofighter Typhoon fighter fleet. Germany’s defense minister Boris Pistorius said during his visit to Australia on Mar. 27, 2026, that Berlin is considering Boeing Defence...

Germany's evaluation of the MQ-28 Ghost Bat reflects NATO's shift toward autonomous combat aircraft to complement manned fighters, signaling accelerated adoption of crewed-uncrewed teaming in European air forces.

See The Aviationist →

Commercial Mar 28, 2026

Dance For First Class? Delta Air Lines Gate Agent Holds Competition In Salt Lake City

Two passengers at Salt Lake City International Airport found themselves in a dance-off to win first class upgrades on a Delta Air Lines flight.

Delta's gamified upgrade strategy transforms gate agents into experiential marketers, leveraging social media moments to build brand loyalty beyond traditional revenue management.

See Simple Flying →

▶
General Aviation Mar 29, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Eastern Iowa Airport's multi-use model supports both flight training operations and commercial air service, creating operational complexity that requires careful traffic management and scheduling.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 28, 2026

The 9 Longest Airbus A350 Routes Today

The aircraft offers exceptional long-range capabilities.

The A350's proven long-range performance is enabling airlines to open previously uneconomical ultra-long-haul markets, reducing connection requirements for passengers on the longest routes.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 28, 2026

USMC F-35Cs and USS Tripoli ARG Arrive in the Middle East

A month after the start of the hostilities, the U.S. Marine Corps is increasing its presence in the Middle East with the arrival of a F-35C squadron and the USS Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group. Mar. 28, 2026, marks a month since the U.S. and Israel launched Operations Epic Fury and Roaring Lion,...

The Marine Corps' deployment of F-35C stealth fighters alongside an ARG signals a shift toward carrier-based air superiority capabilities in sustained Middle East operations.

See The Aviationist →

Drones Mar 28, 2026

Robinson Unmanned

Robinson Unmanned (formerly Ascent AeroSystems) is the uncrewed aircraft systems (UAS) business unit of Robinson...

Robinson's UAS division represents a major helicopter manufacturer's strategic pivot toward autonomous systems, potentially reshaping the competitive landscape in commercial drone operations.

Military Mar 28, 2026

Quantum Systems Strengthens Ukraine’s Air Defence Capabilities with 15,000 Interceptor Contract and WIY Drones Investment

In alignment with the defence priorities set out by the Ukrainian Government, Quantum Systems has...

Commercial drone manufacturer Quantum Systems' 15,000-unit interceptor contract signals military adoption of unmanned systems is shifting from niche to mass-production scale.

Safety Mar 29, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway exits at major hubs like LAX require heightened crew vigilance during taxi procedures, as intersection geometry and signage clarity directly impact incursion risk during high-traffic periods.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Regulatory Mar 28, 2026

Singapore delays SAF levy rollout amid Middle East conflict

As the war in Iran continues to roil world oil markets, Singapore has delayed the rollout of its…...

Geopolitical instability affecting crude oil prices is forcing governments to recalibrate aviation fuel tax implementation timelines, signaling broader economic sensitivity in air transport planning.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 28, 2026

Artemis 2 astronauts arrive at KSC

The four Artemis 2 astronauts arrived in Florida March 27 for final preparations ahead of a launch still scheduled as soon as April 1.

The four-person crew's arrival signals final vehicle integration and launch readiness checks are underway for NASA's crewed lunar return mission.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 27, 2026

[Updated] Iranian Attack on Prince Sultan Air Base Reportedly Struck U.S. KC-135s, E-3 Sentry

Satellite images show signs of an attack at Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia, with The Wall Street Journal now reporting that an Iranian missile damaged “several U.S. refueling aircraft.” Satellite images emerged on Mar. 27, 2026, appeared to show signs of damage on an apron used by U.S....

Direct hits on critical tanker and AWACS aircraft represent a significant degradation of U.S. air operations capability in the Middle East theater.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

I Am Artemis: Erik Richards

Listen to this audio excerpt from Erik Richards, Near Space Network Mission Manager: For Erik Richards, supporting NASA’s first crewed Artemis mission to the Moon and back is the culmination of a career spent helping spacecraft communicate with Earth. Like many kids who grew up at the height of the...

Deep space communication infrastructure built over decades of unmanned missions now enables NASA's return to lunar exploration, making legacy ground network expertise critical to Artemis success.

See NASA →

Industry Mar 29, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operators can significantly reduce environmental impact through operational optimization rather than waiting for new aircraft technology, creating immediate competitive and regulatory advantages.

See MDPI →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon

NASA has awarded Intuitive Machines of Houston, $180.4 million to deliver NASA-funded science and technology to the lunar surface as part of the agency’s CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) initiative and Artemis program. This lunar delivery, which includes seven payloads — five of them NASA’s...

NASA's $180.4M contract to Intuitive Machines demonstrates accelerating commercialization of lunar logistics, positioning private vendors as critical infrastructure for sustained exploration beyond initial crewed Artemis missions.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NASA’s Environment and Energy “Blue Marble” Awards Categories

Category I: NASA Environmental Quality Award Recognizes excellence in environmental management and planning, including stewardship of natural and cultural resources. This category highlights achievements in compliance, conservation, remediation, communication, and environmental information...

NASA's environmental awards recognize that aerospace operations require demonstrating measurable compliance and resource stewardship, setting industry standards for sustainability accountability.

See NASA →

Safety Mar 27, 2026

EASA warns non-airworthy stolen engine parts could re-enter the market

The European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) is warning airlines, maintenance providers and parts buyers to be on…...

Stolen engine components from aircraft maintenance facilities pose immediate safety risks if unknowingly installed, requiring enhanced supply chain verification procedures across the industry.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Two New Associate Members

March 27, 2026 – Washington, D.C.—The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) is pleased to welcome Astrolab and Zeno Power, two innovative companies advancing planetary mobility and reliable energy in extreme environments. […]...

CSF's expansion to include lunar mobility and extreme-environment power systems signals the federation's pivot toward supporting in-situ space infrastructure beyond traditional launch services.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

ESA to decide by June on Europe’s Gateway contributions

MUNICH — For more than a year, questions have swirled about Artemis changes and the Gateway’s role in U.S. lunar ambitions. Following NASA’s March 24 decision to halt work on Gateway, the lunar-orbiting station that had been intended to support astronauts before and after lunar surface missions,...

Europe's Gateway funding decision faces uncertainty after NASA's March halt, potentially reshaping international lunar exploration partnerships and ESA's broader space strategy.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 27, 2026

Gallery: Luke Days 2026

A glimpse back at last weekend's air show at Luke Air Force Base in Arizona.

Luke Air Force Base's air show demonstrates the U.S. Air Force's commitment to community engagement and public display of advanced fighter aircraft operations capabilities.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NISAR’s View of Mount Rainier

Description This image captured by U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR on Nov. 10, 2025, shows Washington’s Mount Rainier. The image is cropped from a much larger swath spanning the Pacific Northwest on a cloudy day; NISAR’s L-band SAR instrument is able to peer through the clouds at the surface...

NISAR's L-band radar penetrates cloud cover to monitor volcanic terrain, enabling continuous Earth observation capabilities for hazard detection regardless of weather conditions.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NISAR Views Mount St. Helens

Description This image captured by U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR on Nov. 10, 2025, shows Washington’s Mount St. Helens. The image is cropped from a much larger swath spanning the Pacific Northwest on a cloudy day; NISAR’s L-band SAR instrument is able to peer through the clouds at the surface...

NISAR's L-band synthetic aperture radar enables all-weather Earth observation through cloud cover, demonstrating critical capabilities for monitoring volcanic activity and environmental changes that inform aviation hazard assessment.

See NASA →

Safety Mar 27, 2026

NTSB Issues Preliminary Report on Hudson River Ditching

Investigators say an instructional flight ended after a loss of oil pressure and total engine failure.

Total engine failure due to oil pressure loss suggests a maintenance or pre-flight inspection gap that warrants review of trainer aircraft condition monitoring protocols.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NASA Tech and Science Bound for Low Earth Orbit on Commercial Launch

Technology and science demonstrations, supported by various NASA industry collaborations and agency developments, are set to launch to low Earth orbit aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket as part of the company’s Transporter-16 commercial rideshare mission. These demonstrations will test thermal...

NASA's reliance on commercial rideshare missions reduces launch costs while accelerating technology validation cycles for space-based experiments.

See NASA →

Regulatory Mar 27, 2026

House Transportation Chair Sam Graves To Retire

Graves helped shape FAA reauthorization, BasicMed and several other general aviation measures.

Graves' retirement removes a key advocate for general aviation policy from Congress, potentially slowing momentum on pending FAA reforms and pilot accessibility initiatives.

See AVweb →
